CREATE TABLE time_zones (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR, abbrev VARCHAR);
SELECT name, abbrev FROM time_zones WHERE abbrev LIKE 'E%';
SELECT name, abbrev FROM time_zones WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM time_zones WHERE abbrev = 'JST');
SELECT tz1.name, tz2.name FROM time_zones tz1 JOIN time_zones tz2 ON tz1.id <> tz2.id WHERE tz1.abbrev = 'EST' AND tz2.abbrev = 'PST';
SELECT tz1.name AS tz1, tz2.name AS tz2 FROM time_zones tz1, time_zones tz2 WHERE tz1.abbrev <> tz2.abbrev ORDER BY tz1.name, tz2.name;
SELECT name, abbrev FROM time_zones ORDER BY CASE abbrev WHEN 'PST' THEN 1 WHEN 'EST' THEN 2 WHEN 'GMT' THEN 3 END;
SELECT name, abbrev FROM time_zones WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM (SELECT id FROM time_zones WHERE abbrev = 'JST') AS temp);
CREATE TABLE temp_zones AS SELECT * FROM time_zones WHERE abbrev LIKE 'A%';
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM time_zones WHERE abbrev IN (SELECT abbrev FROM temp_zones);
SELECT DISTINCT tz1.name FROM time_zones tz1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT * FROM time_zones tz2 WHERE tz1.abbrev <> tz2.abbrev);
